template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS LETTER OF INTENT (REAL ESTATE)**:
A Letter of Intent ('LOI') is a preliminary instrument that sets forth the material business terms of a proposed real estate transaction, typically executed in commercial purchase, lease, or joint-venture transactions PRIOR to the Purchase and Sale Agreement or lease. Massachusetts treats the binding vs. non-binding character of an LOI as a question of the parties' intent — controlling cases are Goren v. Royal Investments, Inc., 25 Mass. App. Ct. 137 (1987) (LOI bound parties where essential terms were agreed and intent was to be bound) and Situation Management Systems, Inc. v. Malouf, Inc., 430 Mass. 875 (2000) (LOI does NOT bind where intent to be bound is explicitly deferred to a later integrated writing). An LOI should therefore STATE EXPRESSLY whether it is binding or non-binding, and if mixed — which provisions are binding (typically confidentiality, exclusivity, expense allocation, governing law) and which are non-binding (price, closing date, operational terms). Target length: 2–4 pages.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `transactionType`: `purchase` (default — acquisition of real property) | `commercial_lease` | `joint_venture` | `development_deal`.
- `bindingNature`: `non_binding_with_carve_outs` (default — operative terms non-binding; confidentiality/exclusivity/expenses binding) | `fully_non_binding` (declaratory only) | `fully_binding` (rare in LOIs; functionally a short-form contract).
- `includeExclusivity`: boolean — when true, include a binding 'no-shop' / exclusivity period.
- `includeConfidentiality`: boolean (default true) — mutual confidentiality of the transaction and terms.
- `includeDueDiligencePeriod`: boolean — include a defined due diligence period with access rights.
- `partyType`: `individual` | `corporate` | `llc` | `trust` — affects signature block.
- `dealStage`: `initial_offer` | `counter_offer` | `post_negotiation` — affects tone and specificity.

**STRUCTURE**:

[Letterhead — sender firm/entity]

[Date]

[Recipient full legal name]
[Recipient title, if applicable]
[Recipient address]

**Re: Letter of Intent — [Property address / Transaction description]**

Dear [Recipient]:

This Letter of Intent ('LOI') sets forth the principal business terms of the proposed [purchase / lease / joint venture] of the real property located at [property address], [city/town], [County] County, Massachusetts (the 'Property'), by and between [Buyer/Tenant/JV Partner A full legal name] ('[Party A Short Name]') and [Seller/Landlord/JV Partner B full legal name] ('[Party B Short Name]') (collectively, the 'Parties').

### 1. PROPERTY DESCRIPTION
The Property consists of [physical description — size, lot, improvements], commonly known as [address], and is more fully described in [recorded deed at Book/Page OR survey attached].

### 2. PRINCIPAL BUSINESS TERMS
[Based on `transactionType`]:

**Purchase transaction:**
| Term | Proposed |
|------|----------|
| Purchase Price | $[amount] |
| Earnest Money Deposit | $[amount], in escrow with [escrow agent] |
| Due Diligence Period | [X] days from execution of PSA |
| Closing Date | [approximate date, subject to PSA] |
| Contingencies | Mortgage, inspection, title, [other] |
| Broker Commission | Seller pays [%]% of Purchase Price |
| Property Condition | 'As-is' subject to inspection / 'With repairs' as specified |

**Commercial lease transaction:**
| Term | Proposed |
|------|----------|
| Leased Premises | [description + square footage] |
| Lease Term | [years], commencing [date] |
| Renewal Options | [number × years] |
| Base Rent | $[amount] per year, net / gross / modified gross |
| Annual Escalations | [fixed % or CPI] |
| Operating Expenses | Pro rata share of [taxes / insurance / CAM] |
| Security Deposit | [amount or 'N/A'] |
| Build-Out / Tenant Improvement | Landlord allowance of $[amount] / per sf |
| Permitted Use | [specific use] |
| Exclusivity | [radius / use restriction] |

**Joint venture / development:**
| Term | Proposed |
|------|----------|
| Equity Contributions | Party A: $[amount] / Party B: [property contribution at $[amount] value] |
| Ownership Percentages | [X]% / [Y]% |
| Management / Control | [Party A as manager / joint manager / majority consent] |
| Distributions Waterfall | Priority return to [party], then pro-rata |
| Major Decisions | Requiring supermajority: [list] |
| Exit / Buy-Sell | Shotgun / right of first refusal / drag-along |

### 3. DEFINITIVE AGREEMENT
The Parties intend to negotiate in good faith toward the execution of a definitive [Purchase and Sale Agreement / Lease / Joint Venture Agreement] (the 'Definitive Agreement') incorporating these principal terms and containing such additional terms as are customary in Massachusetts commercial real estate transactions. The Parties anticipate executing the Definitive Agreement on or before [target date].

### 4. [IF includeDueDiligencePeriod] DUE DILIGENCE
The Parties contemplate a due diligence period of [X] days, commencing upon execution of the Definitive Agreement, during which [Party A] shall have reasonable access to the Property for inspections, and access to the following materials from [Party B]: (a) existing surveys, plans, and title work; (b) environmental reports; (c) leases, subleases, and service contracts; (d) tax bills, MLCs (Municipal Lien Certificates under M.G.L. c. 60, § 23), and utility bills; (e) Title 5 reports where applicable (M.G.L. c. 111, § 127A); (f) lead paint compliance records (M.G.L. c. 111, § 197A) where pre-1978.

### 5. [IF includeExclusivity] EXCLUSIVITY (BINDING)
For a period of [X, typically 30–60] days from the date of this LOI, [Party B] agrees NOT to solicit, negotiate with, enter into, or continue negotiations with any other party regarding the Property (the 'Exclusivity Period'). This provision shall be BINDING on the Parties upon execution. Breach entitles the non-breaching party to injunctive relief and reasonable attorneys' fees.

### 6. [IF includeConfidentiality] CONFIDENTIALITY (BINDING)
The existence and terms of this LOI, and all information exchanged in connection with the proposed transaction, shall be treated as confidential and not disclosed to any third party except: (a) to the Parties' attorneys, accountants, brokers, and financial advisors with a need to know; (b) as required by law; or (c) with the written consent of the other Party. This provision shall be BINDING and shall survive termination of this LOI for [12 / 24] months.

### 7. EXPENSES (BINDING)
Each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses, including attorneys' fees, incurred in connection with this LOI and the negotiation of the Definitive Agreement, regardless of whether the transaction closes.

### 8. NON-BINDING NATURE (EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY STATED)
[Based on `bindingNature`]:

**non_binding_with_carve_outs (default):**
> Except for Sections 5 (Exclusivity), 6 (Confidentiality), 7 (Expenses), 9 (Governing Law), and 10 (Entire Agreement), which are intended to be LEGALLY BINDING on the Parties, this LOI is NON-BINDING and reflects the Parties' current intent only. No contractual obligation with respect to the substantive transaction terms shall arise unless and until the Parties execute a Definitive Agreement. Consistent with Situation Management Systems, Inc. v. Malouf, Inc., 430 Mass. 875 (2000), the Parties' mutual intent is that this LOI shall NOT bind either Party with respect to the purchase price, lease terms, or operational provisions pending execution of a Definitive Agreement.

**fully_non_binding:**
> This LOI is NON-BINDING in its entirety and reflects a preliminary expression of interest only. No contractual obligation shall arise unless and until the Parties execute a Definitive Agreement. The Parties specifically acknowledge that the principles articulated in Situation Management Systems, Inc. v. Malouf, Inc., 430 Mass. 875 (2000), govern: no binding contract exists until the Definitive Agreement is signed.

**fully_binding:**
> This LOI is intended to be LEGALLY BINDING on the Parties as a short-form contract. Execution of the Definitive Agreement shall restate and supersede this LOI without altering the Parties' obligations set forth herein.

### 9. GOVERNING LAW (BINDING)
This LOI, including the binding provisions, shall be governed by and construed under the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. Any dispute arising hereunder shall be brought in the state or federal courts located in [County] County, Massachusetts.

### 10. ENTIRE AGREEMENT (BINDING)
This LOI constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior discussions and correspondence. Modifications must be in writing and signed by both Parties.

### 11. TERMINATION
This LOI shall automatically terminate upon the earlier of: (a) execution of a Definitive Agreement; (b) [X, typically 60–90] days from the date of execution; or (c) mutual written agreement of the Parties. Sections 6 (Confidentiality) and 9 (Governing Law) shall survive termination.

### ACCEPTANCE

If the foregoing accurately reflects your understanding of our mutual intent, please sign below to confirm and return a copy to the undersigned.

Sincerely,

[Party A full legal name]
By: _______________________________
Name: [Authorized signer]
Title: [Title, if entity]
Date: ________________

**Accepted and agreed:**

[Party B full legal name]
By: _______________________________
Name: [Authorized signer]
Title: [Title, if entity]
Date: ________________

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite Massachusetts case law exactly: `Situation Management Systems, Inc. v. Malouf, Inc., 430 Mass. 875 (2000)` (LOI non-binding absent intent to be bound) and `Goren v. Royal Investments, Inc., 25 Mass. App. Ct. 137 (1987)` (LOI can bind when essential terms are definite and parties intend to be bound).
- Cite M.G.L. where relevant: `c. 259, § 1` (Statute of Frauds — a binding real estate LOI MUST satisfy the writing requirement for the transfer of an interest in land), `c. 60, § 23` (MLC), `c. 111, § 127A` (Title 5), `c. 111, § 197A` (lead paint).
- The BINDING vs. NON-BINDING distinction is the single most important drafting decision in an LOI. Always state explicitly — ambiguity has produced significant Massachusetts litigation.
- Under Massachusetts law, a writing sufficient to satisfy the Statute of Frauds (c. 259, § 1) must identify the parties, the property, and the price. An LOI with these elements and no disclaimer CAN bind the parties, consistent with Goren. Always include the disclaimer unless the parties intend full-binding.
- Exclusivity and confidentiality provisions are STANDARD binding carve-outs; do not omit unless the parties intend a purely indicative document.
- For commercial transactions, LOIs are routinely executed; for residential transactions, the Offer to Purchase (OTP) serves this function in Massachusetts — and the OTP IS binding under McCarthy v. Tobin, 429 Mass. 84 (1999). Do not conflate an OTP with an LOI.
- Target length: 2–4 pages.
hints: Massachusetts Real Estate Letter of Intent. Binding vs. non-binding is the critical drafting decision. Controlling cases: Situation Management Systems, Inc. v. Malouf, Inc., 430 Mass. 875 (2000) (non-binding when intent deferred) vs. Goren v. Royal Investments, 25 Mass. App. Ct. 137 (1987) (LOI binding when essential terms agreed + intent to bind). Statute of Frauds per c. 259 § 1 — an LOI for real estate with definite terms CAN meet the writing requirement, so the disclaimer matters. Standard binding carve-outs: exclusivity, confidentiality, expenses, governing law. Residential transactions use Offer to Purchase (binding per McCarthy v. Tobin, 429 Mass. 84 (1999)) — LOIs are for commercial / JV / development deals. Reference DD materials: MLC (c. 60 § 23), Title 5 (c. 111 § 127A), lead paint (c. 111 § 197A).
